summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function project. The solution queries GPT-4o via TRAPI (no API keys — Managed Identity auth) to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster, stores the result to Azure Blob Storage, and runs nightly via an Azure Functions Timer Trigger.
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the internal TRAPI (Trusted Request API / Azure AI proxy) that supports Managed Identity authentication to GPT-4o, satisfying the 'No API Keys' security requirement.
The Azure Function will be written in Python (most common for AI/data pipeline functions) unless the team specifies otherwise.
Azure Blob Storage (standard LRS) is sufficient for storing the roster JSON output.
A Consumption or Flex Consumption Azure Functions hosting plan is appropriate given the nightly, low-frequency execution.
A Timer Trigger cron expression of '0 0 2 * * *' (2 AM UTC daily) will be used unless the team specifies a different time.
GitHub Actions will be used for CI/CD.
Bicep will be used for Infrastructure as Code.
A single Azure resource group will be created for all resources.
Application Insights will be used for observability.
The blob output will be a JSON file named with the execution date (e.g., 1985-yankees-2026-04-02.json) and stored in a container named 'yankees-roster'.
The plan is not auto-approved; manual review is required.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint and tenant/subscription scope for GPT-4o access?
Which Azure subscription and region should resources be deployed to?
Should the blob be overwritten nightly or versioned/appended with a date suffix?
Is there a specific Azure Functions runtime version (v2 Python model vs v1) required?
What is the preferred nightly execution time (UTC)?
Are there network restrictions (private endpoints, VNet integration) required?
What is the target Azure environment (dev/staging/prod) and promotion strategy?
Should alert notifications be sent to a Teams channel, email, or PagerDuty?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish infrastructure, identity, secrets-free auth, and CI/CD scaffolding so all subsequent feature work has a deployable target.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and open questions triaged
Azure subscription and resource group name confirmed
TRAPI endpoint and Managed Identity requirements confirmed with platform team
exit_criteria: Azure resource group, Storage Account, and Function App provisioned via Bicep
Managed Identity assigned with required RBAC roles (Storage Blob Data Contributor, TRAPI access)
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline deploys to Function App successfully
No secrets or API keys in any committed file

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the Azure Function with Timer Trigger, TRAPI/GPT-4o integration, and Blob Storage output.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint confirmed and reachable from Function App Managed Identity
Blob Storage container created and accessible
exit_criteria: Timer Trigger Function executes on schedule and completes without error
GPT-4o via TRAPI returns 1985 Yankees roster data
Roster JSON written to Azure Blob Storage container
Unit tests pass in CI for all new modules
PRs for APP-01, APP-02, APP-03, INF-04 merged and reviewed by @copilot

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, observability, error handling, and security controls to production-harden the solution.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Structured logs emitted to Application Insights on each execution
Alert fired on function failure (tested via forced failure)
Security review checklist completed — no secrets, least-privilege RBAC confirmed
PRs for OPS-01, OPS-02, SEC-02, REL-02 merged and reviewed by @copilot

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end execution, complete documentation, and confirm production readiness.
entry_criteria: All unit and integration tests passing in CI
exit_criteria: End-to-end nightly run validated in production (or production-equivalent) environment
All 1985 Yankees roster members present in output blob
Production deployment confirmed and monitored for one full nightly cycle

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this implementation task plan for completeness, sequencing, and alignment with the spec. Resolve open questions (TRAPI endpoint, Azure subscription, region, nightly schedule time) before foundation work begins.
outputs: Resolved open questions documented in repo
acceptance_criteria: All open questions in this plan have documented answers in the repository
Task plan has been reviewed and approved by the project owner (rciapala)
GitHub issues created for all tasks in this plan
Labels applied per task definition
risks: Unresolved TRAPI endpoint or Managed Identity scope will block INF-02 and APP-01

tasks.title: Provision Azure resource group and core infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author Bicep templates to provision the Azure Resource Group, Azure Storage Account (with 'yankees-roster' blob container), Azure Function App (Consumption plan, Python runtime), and Application Insights. All resources should use consistent naming and tagging conventions.
outputs: Bicep templates committed to repo under /infra
Azure resources provisioned in target subscription
acceptance_criteria: Bicep templates are idempotent and deploy without errors via 'az deployment group create'
Azure Storage Account provisioned with 'yankees-roster' blob container
Azure Function App provisioned with Python runtime on Consumption plan
Application Insights instance linked to Function App
All resources tagged with project name '1985-NY-Yankees' and owner 'rciapala'
No secrets or connection strings stored in Bicep or parameter files
Infrastructure validation: 'az resource list --resource-group <rg>' shows all expected resources
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Quota limits on Azure subscription may delay provisioning
Region availability for Consumption plan Functions

tasks.title: Configure System-Assigned Managed Identity and RBAC for Function App
tasks.description: Enable System-Assigned Managed Identity on the Azure Function App. Assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role on the Storage Account and any required TRAPI/Azure AI access role to the identity. No API keys or connection strings should be used.
inputs: TRAPI access role requirements from platform team
outputs: Managed Identity enabled on Function App
RBAC role assignments applied via Bicep or CLI
TRAPI access confirmed for Function App identity
acceptance_criteria: Function App has System-Assigned Managed Identity enabled (visible in Azure Portal > Identity)
'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role assigned to Function App identity on the Storage Account
TRAPI/Azure OpenAI access role assigned to Function App identity
No storage connection strings or API keys present in Function App configuration or code
Infrastructure validation: 'az role assignment list --assignee <principalId>' shows expected roles
A test script or manual verification confirms the identity can write a test blob without a connection string
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: TRAPI may require a specific Azure AD app role or custom RBAC — confirm with platform team before implementation
Managed Identity propagation delay (up to 5 minutes) may cause transient auth failures during initial testing

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Function App deployment
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests, lints the Python code, and deploys the Azure Function App on merge to main. Use OIDC (Workload Identity Federation) for Azure authentication — no service principal secrets.
inputs: Azure subscription and Function App name
outputs: OIDC federation configured between GitHub repo and Azure
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on push to main and on pull_request
Unit test step runs and fails the workflow if tests fail
Lint step (flake8 or ruff) runs and fails on errors
Deployment step uses OIDC (azure/login with client-id, tenant-id, subscription-id) — no client secrets
Successful deployment verified by checking Function App status via 'az functionapp show'
Infrastructure validation: pipeline runs end-to-end green on a test commit
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: OIDC federation setup requires Azure AD app registration — confirm permissions with Azure AD admin
GitHub Actions runner must have network access to Azure management endpoints

tasks.title: Establish secrets-free configuration and security baseline
tasks.description: Define and enforce a secrets-free configuration policy: all Azure service access uses Managed Identity, TRAPI access uses Azure AD token (no API keys), and no credentials appear in code, config files, or environment variables. Add a pre-commit hook or CI check to scan for secrets.
outputs: Secret scanning check in CI (e.g., trufflehog or gitleaks step)
CONTRIBUTING.md or README section documenting no-API-key policy
Azure Function App settings reviewed for any plaintext secrets
acceptance_criteria: CI pipeline includes a secret scanning step that fails on detected secrets
Function App configuration (app settings) contains no API keys, passwords, or connection strings
All Azure SDK clients in application code use DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity)
Security checklist documented and sign-off recorded in PR
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: TRAPI SDK or library may default to API key auth — verify it supports Azure AD token / DefaultAzureCredential

tasks.title: Define Function App baseline configuration and app settings
tasks.description: Configure required Azure Function App application settings: Python version, AzureWebJobsStorage (using Managed Identity / __BLOBSERVICEURI__), FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME, Application Insights connection string, TRAPI endpoint URL, and blob container name. All sensitive values use Key Vault references or Managed Identity — no plaintext secrets.
outputs: App settings defined in Bicep parameters or Azure Portal
Local.settings.json template (no secrets) committed to repo
acceptance_criteria: AzureWebJobsStorage configured using identity-based connection (no connection string)
TRAPI_ENDPOINT app setting present and points to correct endpoint
BLOB_CONTAINER_NAME app setting set to 'yankees-roster'
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING set (non-secret, instrumentation key only)
local.settings.json.template committed with placeholder values and no real secrets
Infrastructure validation: Function App starts without errors after settings applied
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage requires Function App runtime 4.x+ — confirm runtime version

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o integration to retrieve 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that calls GPT-4o via the TRAPI endpoint using Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential / azure-identity) to prompt for the complete 1985 New York Yankees roster. The module must return a structured list of player names and positions.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and API schema
acceptance_criteria: Module authenticates to TRAPI using DefaultAzureCredential — no hardcoded API keys
GPT-4o prompt requests the 1985 NY Yankees complete roster (all players)
Response is parsed into a structured Python list/dict of player name and position
Unit tests mock the TRAPI HTTP call and assert correct parsing of a sample response
Unit tests cover at least: successful response, empty response, HTTP error response
All unit tests pass in CI
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: GPT-4o may hallucinate or return an incomplete roster — consider a validation step or known-player cross-check
TRAPI token acquisition may fail in local dev without a Managed Identity — document local dev auth (az login fallback)

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage write module for roster output
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that serializes the roster list to JSON and writes it to the 'yankees-roster' Azure Blob Storage container using the azure-storage-blob SDK with DefaultAzureCredential. The blob name should include the execution date (e.g., 1985-yankees-YYYY-MM-DD.json).
acceptance_criteria: Module uses DefaultAzureCredential to authenticate to Storage — no connection strings
Blob is written to 'yankees-roster' container with date-stamped name
Blob content is valid JSON containing the full roster list
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient and assert correct blob name, container, and content
Unit tests cover: successful write, storage service error
All unit tests pass in CI
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Managed Identity RBAC propagation delay may cause auth errors in early testing — retry with backoff

tasks.title: Implement Azure Function Timer Trigger orchestrating nightly execution
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function entry point as a Timer Trigger function (cron: '0 0 2 * * *' UTC or as confirmed). The function calls the TRAPI client module to retrieve the roster, then calls the blob writer module to store it. Include structured logging at each step.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger cron expression matches confirmed nightly schedule
Function calls trapi_client and blob_writer in sequence
Structured log messages emitted at: function start, roster retrieved (with count), blob written, function complete
Function logs and raises exceptions on failure (does not silently swallow errors)
Unit tests mock trapi_client and blob_writer and assert orchestration logic
Unit tests cover: happy path, TRAPI failure, blob write failure
All unit tests pass in CI
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Timer Trigger cold start on Consumption plan may add latency — acceptable for nightly batch
If GPT-4o response is slow, function may approach default 5-minute timeout — monitor execution duration

tasks.title: Provision infrastructure for nightly Timer Trigger schedule validation
tasks.description: Update Bicep templates to ensure the Function App's WEBSITE_TIME_ZONE or cron expression aligns with the confirmed nightly schedule. Verify that the AzureWebJobsStorage identity-based connection supports Timer Trigger lease management (required for distributed lock).
outputs: Updated Bicep with schedule-related app settings
Validation that timer fires at expected time in deployed environment
acceptance_criteria: WEBSITE_TIME_ZONE or cron uses UTC and matches confirmed schedule
Timer Trigger fires at the expected time in the deployed Function App (verified via Application Insights logs)
AzureWebJobsStorage identity-based connection successfully manages timer leases (no 'failed to acquire lease' errors)
Infrastructure validation: Azure Monitor / Application Insights shows timer invocation at correct time
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage for Timer Trigger lease management may require specific SDK versions

tasks.title: Implement structured logging and Application Insights telemetry
tasks.description: Ensure all function execution events (start, roster size, blob write success/failure, duration) are emitted as structured logs to Application Insights. Configure log level and ensure correlation IDs propagate across the TRAPI call and blob write.
inputs: Application Insights connection string in app settings
outputs: Structured log statements in all modules
Application Insights dashboard or query showing nightly execution logs
acceptance_criteria: Logs appear in Application Insights under 'traces' for each execution
Log fields include: execution_date, player_count, blob_name, duration_ms, status
Errors appear in Application Insights 'exceptions' table with stack trace
Kusto query confirmed to retrieve last 7 days of executions
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Application Insights SDK may require explicit configuration for Python Azure Functions v2 model

tasks.title: Configure alerting for function execution failures
tasks.description: Create an Azure Monitor alert rule that fires when the Timer Trigger function fails (exception count > 0) or does not execute within the expected nightly window. Route alerts to the confirmed notification channel (Teams webhook or email).
outputs: Azure Monitor alert rule (Bicep or Portal)
Test alert fired and received on notification channel
acceptance_criteria: Alert rule created for function exception count > 0 with 5-minute evaluation window
Alert rule created for missing nightly execution (timer did not fire in expected window)
Alert routes to confirmed notification channel
Test: forced function failure triggers alert within 10 minutes
Alert rules defined in Bicep for repeatability
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Alert notification channel (Teams webhook or email group) must be confirmed before implementation

tasks.title: Security review: least-privilege RBAC and no-secrets audit
tasks.description: Conduct a security review to verify that the Function App Managed Identity has only the minimum required RBAC roles, no secrets exist in code or configuration, and the TRAPI integration uses token-based auth. Document findings and remediate any issues.
acceptance_criteria: Managed Identity has only 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' on the target Storage Account (not subscription-wide)
No API keys, passwords, or connection strings in Function App settings, code, or Git history
TRAPI client uses DefaultAzureCredential exclusively
Secret scanning CI check passes with zero findings
Security review sign-off recorded in PR
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Overly broad RBAC roles (e.g., Contributor on subscription) may have been granted during early testing

tasks.title: Implement retry and error handling for TRAPI and Blob Storage calls
tasks.description: Add retry logic (exponential backoff, max 3 retries) for transient TRAPI HTTP errors and Azure Storage errors. Ensure the function raises a terminal exception after exhausting retries so Azure Functions runtime marks the execution as failed (enabling alerting).
outputs: Retry logic in trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff on HTTP 429, 500, 502, 503
Blob writer retries up to 3 times on transient storage errors
After max retries, exception is raised and propagates to function host
Unit tests simulate transient errors and verify retry count and final exception
host.json does not configure function-level retries that would conflict with module-level retries
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: GPT-4o via TRAPI may have rate limits that require longer backoff intervals — confirm with platform team

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test and nightly execution validation
tasks.description: Trigger a manual execution of the deployed Azure Function and verify the complete end-to-end flow: TRAPI returns roster, blob is written with correct content, logs appear in Application Insights, and alert rules are healthy. Validate the blob contains all known 1985 Yankees players.
inputs: Known 1985 Yankees player list for validation
acceptance_criteria: Manual trigger via Azure Portal or CLI executes the function without error
Blob appears in 'yankees-roster' container with correct date-stamped name
Blob JSON contains a complete list of 1985 NY Yankees players (cross-referenced against known roster)
Application Insights shows execution trace with player_count > 0
No exceptions recorded in Application Insights for the test run
Nightly timer fires at least once and produces correct output (verified next business day)
Test results documented in PR or repo wiki
risks: GPT-4o may return a slightly different roster on different runs — establish an expected minimum player count (e.g., 25 players)
Nightly execution validation requires waiting until the next scheduled trigger

tasks.title: Write README and operational runbook
tasks.description: Write a README.md covering project purpose, architecture overview, local development setup (using az login for DefaultAzureCredential), deployment steps, and a runbook section covering how to check execution logs, re-trigger manually, and respond to alerts.
acceptance_criteria: README includes: project overview, architecture diagram or description, prerequisites, local dev setup, deployment instructions
Runbook covers: how to check last execution in Application Insights, how to manually trigger the function, how to respond to failure alerts
No secrets or real credentials in any documentation
README reviewed and approved in PR
PR created with @copilot assigned for review

tasks.title: Production deployment and go-live sign-off
tasks.description: Deploy the final validated function to the production environment, confirm the next nightly execution fires successfully, and obtain go-live sign-off from the project owner (rciapala). Ensure monitoring is active and the runbook is accessible.
acceptance_criteria: Production Function App deployed via CI/CD pipeline (not manual portal deployment)
First nightly execution in production completes successfully
Roster blob written to production Storage Account
Application Insights shows successful execution with no exceptions
Alert rules active and confirmed healthy in Azure Monitor
Go-live sign-off recorded (PR comment, email, or issue comment from rciapala)
risks: Production Managed Identity may need separate RBAC assignments from dev/staging — verify before go-live
TRAPI quota limits in production environment may differ from dev
